title: podman-remote ssh:<domain>:22 connection issue
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When adding a system connection for use with podman-remote the added connection adds ':22' (without quotes) to the ssh connection DOMAIN. This seems to cause a problem with my DNS lookup, and ssh connection fails.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman-remote system connection add NAME --identity IDENTITY ssh://USER@DOMAIN/run/podman/podman.socket
2. podman-remote system connection list
   ... ssh://USER@DOMAIN:22/run/podman/podman.socket

**Describe the results you received:**
podman-remote ps -a
   ssh: rejected: connect failed

**Describe the results you expected:**
podman-remote ps -a
  ...list of containers

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I can successfully ssh into DOMAIN without password. I am unsuccessful using DOMAIN:22 as the DNS lookup fails.

Podman socket is running on server.

I tried:
host DOMAIN:22
- unknown.

host DOMAIN
- found.

I cannot avoid having the ':22' added to the end of the DOMAIN.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes 4.1.1. No to second question.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Bare Metal

comments.body:
we default to adding port 22 to the end of domains if no other port is provided. I think a port is necessary and 22 should work as it is the default port for ssh connections. 

if you have a custom setup you need to figure out which port is exposed for ssh and specify that when you add the connection.

This will also change when I add the new ssh engine which will allow you to ditch golang's quirks (which this is one of)

Hi thanks for looking at this. Can I suggest that this is a DNS issue because the DOMAIN is not separated from the port when used in the code?

BTW I have not looked at the podman code yet. I might need some pointers on where to look.

 tried:
host DOMAIN:22

    unknown.

host DOMAIN

    found.

because this is the error:

podman-remote ps -a
ssh: rejected: connect failed

That may mean that you can take your time changing over from golang's ssh engine.

Anyways that was my first though, as I can ssh into the remote fine until I add :22 to the domain. Surely others have seen this?
@orangutanga try using `podman --ssh=native...` if that still does not work I will reopen this and look into it more. I am pretty sure my patches fixed this in both paths.
